title: Resilient caller with retry semantics and a circuit breaker

difficulty.rationale: Requires correct idempotent-vs-non-idempotent retry handling, a precise consecutive-failure counting model, and a closed/open/half_open state machine with fail-fast behavior and time-based cooldown recovery, all kept deterministic and free of off-by-one errors.

gates.name: security_baseline
gates.requirement: no hidden test access or network egress

must: GET /call retries up to 3 attempts and reports the succeeding attempt number.
POST /call invokes the dependency at most once with no retry.
Five consecutive failed calls open the breaker.
While open, a /call returns 503 without invoking the dependency.
After the cooldown a successful trial closes the breaker and resets failures.
must_not: Invoke the dependency while the breaker is open.
Count retry attempts as separate consecutive failures.
edge_cases: A failed GET /call increments dependency_calls by 3 but consecutive_failures by 1.
A failed half-open trial reopens the breaker and restarts the cooldown.
An invalid dependency mode returns 400 and leaves the current mode intact.

reward_model.description: Harbor reward.txt is computed by the bun-bench gate model in tests/test.sh.
